instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Is E-Insurance? Know The New Draft Guidelines On E-Insurance Policies (New IRDAI Development)
As industries and companies are becoming increasingly digital and offering online offerings, the life insurance industry, too, is making big strides in that direction. E-insurance is gaining traction at a faster rate, with reports indicating that digital insurance sales are expected to make up around 75%1 of new policies in the coming years.
If you are wondering what e-insurance is, the term refers to insurance policies (life insurance and non-life insurance) that have been stored in an electronic form, compared to the conventional paper form. These policies are also referred to as dematerialised policies.
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has recently released draft guidelines2 regarding the dematerialisation of insurance policies. The draft, framed within the IRDAI (Issuance of e-Insurance Policies) Regulations, 20222, states that insurers who sell insurance policies through electronic platforms should have an e-proposal form, along with a physical proposal form.
Let’s take a detailed look at the new guidelines and how they can affect policyholders.
Understanding the e-insurance draft in detail
As per the e-insurance draft2, the objective of this move is, “to promote efficiency in the conduct of insurance business by enabling the insurers to establish an appropriate framework by adopting technology to build capabilities:
a) for solicitation of insurance business through an electronic platform,
b) to issue e-insurance policies procured either through an electronic platform or otherwise.
c) to mandate the use of electronic insurance accounts for holding e-insurance policies.”
The guidelines also declare that the e-proposal form should include the suitability assessment form and the benefit illustration (customised as per the policyholder).
If the implementation of the drafted changes were to happen, every policyholder should have an e-insurance account, be it for an e-term life insurance policy, or an e-whole life plan. So, when you purchase a new e-term life insurance policy, the policy document shall be stored in a dematerialised form in your e-insurance account.
How insurers shall issue an electronic/dematerialised insurance policy
Once you have a fair idea of what e-insurance is, you should move towards understanding the process of issuing a general or life insurance policy in the dematerialised or electronic form, i.e., the dematerialisation of insurance policies. The process shall have the following aspects2:
Collection of the prospective policyholder’s information will be carried out online.
Underwriting of the proposal for the insurance shall be completed.
The policyholder will make an electronic payment for the policy costs after which the e-policy will be issued to them.
As the renewal date for the e-insurance policy nears, an auto-generated renewal notice will be forwarded to the policyholder. This notice shall also include the grace period.
The payment of the renewal premium shall also be carried out electronically.
Other requirements related to policy servicing can also be met digitally, including claims servicing.
Before you engage in such a process, ensure you acquaint yourself very well with what e-insurance is. Also, as with regular policies, it may help to use a life insurance calculator before you make any purchase decisions.
Features2 of an E-Insurance Account
After understanding what e-insurance is and its relevant guidelines, let’s look at the various useful features of an e-insurance account:
Unique e-insurance account (eIA) number
Each policyholder will have a unique e-insurance account number. The mechanism to provide this eIA number shall be taken care of by the insurer. The eIA number once created should be used for policies from all insurers without any changes, as per the guidelines.
Insurance repository
E-insurance account services can be provided only by those entities that have been granted the required certificate. This certification shall register them for storing the data of insurance policies (life and non-life insurance) and policyholders in electronic form on behalf of the insurers.
eIA information to be made available in multiple languages
Once the insurer has created an eIA for the prospect, details regarding the same shall be communicated to them via a registered email ID, or an SMS. Such communication shall explain the purpose of creating the eIA and how to use it in various regional languages, as well as Hindi and English.
Benefits of e-insurance for policyholders
The implementation of the e-insurance guidelines can benefit a policyholder in several ways, such as:
Reduction of policy costs2
As per one of the primary changes proposed in the draft, policyholders can expect insurers to provide a reduction in the policy costs if they buy it directly via electronic means. Such a decrease in premium rates shall be available only as per the applicable limit prescribed by the authorities.
Increased ease and convenience
A policyholder can keep and access several insurance policies in their e-insurance account. It would make it relatively easier for the policyholder to manage several policies. Renewing or porting policies could become more convenient as well.
If the guidelines are to be implemented, the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Issuance of E Policies) Regulations, 2016 shall be repealed2.
References:
1https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/online-insurance-market-in-india#faqs:~:text=account%20for%20more%20than%2075%25%20of%20the%20new%20policies%20over%20the%20next%20few%20years
2https://www.irdai.gov.in/ADMINCMS/cms/frmGeneral_Layout.aspx?page=PageNo4818
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
